name: Business Technology & Digital Literacy
description: Foundational digital literacy for the modern business world — productivity tools, spreadsheets, data basics, internet skills, cybersecurity awareness, and professional digital communication.

modules.title: Digital Literacy Foundations
modules.objectives: Identify hardware and software components; understand operating systems; manage files and folders; navigate the internet safely.
modules.readingNote: Google Digital Garage — Digital Skills for Work: Module 1 (The Online World)
modules.videoNote: GCFGlobal — Computer Basics: What is a Computer?
modules.video2Note: GCFGlobal — Computer Basics: Understanding Operating Systems
modules.practiceNote: Khan Academy — Computer Science Fundamentals: Intro activities
modules.assignment: Label a diagram of a desktop computer identifying at least 8 hardware components. Create a folder structure for a fictional school project using at least 3 levels of organization.

modules.title: Professional Email & Communication
modules.objectives: Write professional emails using correct tone, structure, and etiquette; use CC, BCC, and Reply-All appropriately; understand professional messaging norms.
modules.readingNote: Google Digital Garage — Digital Skills for Work: Email & Communication module
modules.videoNote: GCFGlobal — Email Basics: What is Email?
modules.video2Note: GCFGlobal — Email Tips: How to Write a Professional Email
modules.practiceNote: Khan Academy — The Internet: How email works
modules.assignment: Write 3 professional emails: (1) requesting information from a company, (2) following up after a job interview, (3) apologizing for a missed deadline. Annotate each email identifying subject line, greeting, body, and closing.

modules.title: Word Processing & Document Design
modules.objectives: Use Google Docs or Microsoft Word to create, format, and style documents; apply headings, margins, spacing, and templates; produce a professional report.
modules.readingNote: Google Digital Garage — Digital Skills for Work: Creating Documents
modules.videoNote: GCFGlobal — Google Docs: Getting Started with Google Docs
modules.video2Note: GCFGlobal — Word 2016: Formatting Text and Adding Hyperlinks
modules.practiceNote: Google Docs — Create a document using the provided template and practice formatting styles
modules.assignment: Create a 2-page report on a technology topic of your choice. Apply: title, at least 3 heading levels, body text, bullet lists, a table, and page numbers. Use a consistent font and style throughout.

modules.title: Spreadsheets: Basics
modules.objectives: Enter and format data in Google Sheets or Excel; use basic formulas: SUM, AVERAGE, IF, COUNT; understand cell references.
modules.readingNote: Google Digital Garage — Digital Skills for Work: Spreadsheet Basics
modules.videoNote: GCFGlobal — Excel 2016: Getting Started with Excel
modules.practiceNote: Khan Academy — Intro to programming: practice data entry and logic exercises
modules.assignment: Build a personal budget spreadsheet for one month. Include income, at least 8 expense categories, and use SUM, AVERAGE, and IF formulas. Format with currency, bold headers, and conditional highlighting for over-budget cells.

modules.title: Spreadsheets: Data Analysis
modules.objectives: Sort and filter data; use VLOOKUP; create pivot tables; build charts to visualize data trends.
modules.readingNote: Google Digital Garage — Digital Skills for Work: Using Data in Spreadsheets
modules.video2Note: GCFGlobal — Excel: How to Use VLOOKUP
modules.practiceNote: Khan Academy — Statistics & Probability: Reading and interpreting data displays
modules.assignment: Given a provided dataset of 50 sales records, (1) sort by revenue descending, (2) filter to show only records from Q3, (3) create a VLOOKUP to retrieve product names by ID, (4) build a pivot table summarizing revenue by category, (5) create a bar chart.

modules.title: Presentations & Slide Design
modules.titleZh: 演示文稿与幻灯片设计
modules.objectives: Build effective presentations in Google Slides or PowerPoint; apply design principles: visual hierarchy, color, font, and white space; structure a narrative with slides.
modules.readingNote: Google Digital Garage — Digital Skills for Work: Creating Presentations
modules.video2Note: TED-Ed — What makes a truly great presentation?
modules.practiceNote: Google Slides — Design a 5-slide pitch deck using a custom theme
modules.assignment: Create a 10-slide presentation on a business idea of your choice. Apply: title slide, agenda, 6 content slides, and a closing slide. Use consistent fonts, a color palette of 3 colors, and include at least 2 data visualizations.

modules.title: Cloud Computing & Collaboration
modules.objectives: Use Google Drive or OneDrive to store, share, and collaborate on files; understand version history; apply real-time co-editing best practices.
modules.readingNote: Google Digital Garage — Digital Skills for Work: Working in the Cloud
modules.practiceNote: Google Drive — Organize a shared folder, set permissions, and use version history
modules.assignment: Set up a shared Google Drive folder simulating a team project. Create a shared Doc, Sheet, and Slides file. Practice using comments, suggestions mode, and version history. Write a one-page reflection on the benefits and challenges of cloud collaboration.

modules.title: Internet Research & Information Literacy
modules.objectives: Evaluate sources for credibility and bias; use Boolean search operators; fact-check claims; identify misinformation.
modules.readingNote: Google Digital Garage — Digital Skills for Work: Finding Things Online
modules.videoNote: TED-Ed — How to choose your news
modules.video2Note: TED-Ed — How to spot a misleading graph
modules.practiceNote: Khan Academy — Research skills: evaluating sources practice
modules.assignment: Select 3 online articles on the same news story from different sources. Evaluate each using the SIFT method (Stop, Investigate the source, Find better coverage, Trace claims). Write a 1-page comparison explaining which sources you trust most and why.

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of hardware?
questions.explanation: Hardware refers to the physical components of a computer. A keyboard is a physical input device.

questions.question: Which of the following is the correct structure for a professional email?
options: Subject line, greeting, body, professional closing, signature
questions.answer: Subject line, greeting, body, professional closing, signature
questions.explanation: Professional emails follow the structure: clear subject line, polite greeting, organized body, professional closing (e.g., 'Best regards'), and a signature.

questions.question: In an email, BCC stands for:
questions.explanation: BCC (Blind Carbon Copy) sends a copy of the email to additional recipients without other recipients seeing their addresses.

questions.question: True or False: The answer to "Which Google Docs feature allows you to see and restore a previous version of a document" is: Version History.
questions.explanation: True. Version History is correct.

questions.question: In a spreadsheet, which formula would calculate the total of cells A1 through A10?
questions.explanation: =SUM(A1:A10) adds all numeric values in the range A1 through A10.

questions.question: What does the AVERAGE function do in a spreadsheet?
options: Finds the largest value in a range
Counts the number of cells with data
Calculates the arithmetic mean of a range of numbers
Rounds all values to the nearest integer
questions.answer: Calculates the arithmetic mean of a range of numbers
questions.explanation: =AVERAGE(range) sums all values in the range and divides by the count of those values to return the arithmetic mean.

questions.question: Which design principle means that the most important element on a slide should visually stand out the most?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Visual hierarchy.

questions.question: True or False: The answer to "Which of the following best describes cloud storage" is: Files stored on a USB drive.
questions.explanation: False. The correct answer is: Files stored on remote servers accessible via the internet.

questions.question: Using the Boolean search operator AND in a search engine:
options: Finds pages with either of the terms
Excludes a term from search results
Requires both terms to appear in results
questions.answer: Requires both terms to appear in results
questions.explanation: Boolean AND narrows a search by requiring all connected terms to be present in every result returned.

questions.question: Which characteristic is a red flag that an email might be a phishing attempt?
options: It comes from a known colleague
It asks you to verify your password by clicking a link
It has a formal greeting and signature
It was sent during business hours
questions.answer: It asks you to verify your password by clicking a link
questions.explanation: Legitimate organizations never ask for passwords via email. Urgent requests to click links and enter credentials are classic phishing tactics.

questions.question: Which of the following is the strongest password?
questions.explanation: Strong passwords are long, use a mix of uppercase, lowercase, numbers, and symbols, and avoid common words or personal information.

questions.question: True or False: A digital footprint is "The trail of data you leave behind from your online activities".
questions.explanation: True. The trail of data you leave behind from your online activities is correct.

questions.question: When sharing a Google Drive file, setting access to 'Viewer' means the recipient can:
options: Only view the file without making changes
questions.answer: Only view the file without making changes
questions.explanation: Viewer access allows recipients to see the file contents but not edit, comment, or share it.

questions.question: You receive an email from 'support@paypa1.com' saying your account has been suspended and you must click a link immediately to restore access. Identify two specific red flags in this scenario and explain what you should do.
questions.answer: Red flags: (1) misspelled domain name (paypa1.com uses the number '1' instead of the letter 'l'); (2) urgency/pressure tactic ('immediately'). Action: do not click the link; go directly to the official website by typing the URL; report the email as phishing.
questions.explanation: Phishing emails commonly use lookalike domains and create urgency to trick users into acting before thinking critically.

questions.question: A classmate says, 'Cloud storage is risky — I only save files on my computer.' Provide two advantages of cloud storage over local storage only, and one valid concern your classmate might have.
questions.answer: Advantages: (1) files are accessible from any internet-connected device; (2) automatic backup prevents data loss if the device is lost or damaged. Valid concern: if the internet is unavailable or the cloud provider has an outage, files may be temporarily inaccessible; also, privacy concerns about data stored on third-party servers.
questions.explanation: Cloud storage offers significant convenience and redundancy, but relying solely on the cloud introduces internet dependency and third-party privacy risks.

questions.question: True or False: The answer to "Which component is the 'brain' of a computer that processes instructions" is: RAM.
questions.explanation: False. The correct answer is: CPU.

questions.question: The subject line of a professional email should be:
options: Long and detailed to explain everything
Concise and descriptive of the email's purpose
Written in all capital letters to grab attention
questions.answer: Concise and descriptive of the email's purpose
questions.explanation: A good subject line is brief yet informative, giving the recipient a clear idea of the email's content before opening it.

questions.question: In Microsoft Word or Google Docs, 'Styles' are used to:
options: Apply consistent formatting to headings and body text throughout a document
Convert the document to a PDF
questions.answer: Apply consistent formatting to headings and body text throughout a document
questions.explanation: Styles (Heading 1, Heading 2, Normal, etc.) enforce consistent formatting and allow automatic generation of a table of contents.

questions.question: In a spreadsheet, which formula returns 'Pass' if the value in A1 is 60 or above, and 'Fail' otherwise?
questions.explanation: The IF function evaluates a condition (A1>=60) and returns the first value ('Pass') if true, or the second ('Fail') if false.

questions.question: True or False: The answer to "VLOOKUP in a spreadsheet is primarily used to:" is: Look up a value in a column and return a related value from the same row.
questions.explanation: True. Look up a value in a column and return a related value from the same row is correct.

questions.question: Which chart type is best for showing how a whole is divided into parts (e.g., budget by category)?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Pie chart.

questions.question: What does the '6 × 6 rule' for presentations suggest?
options: Use at least 6 fonts per slide
Use no more than 6 bullet points per slide and 6 words per bullet
Have at least 6 slides with 6 paragraphs each
questions.answer: Use no more than 6 bullet points per slide and 6 words per bullet
questions.explanation: The 6×6 rule is a design guideline to keep slides uncluttered: max 6 bullet points per slide and 6 words per bullet.

questions.question: Real-time collaboration in Google Drive means:
options: Only one person can edit the document at a time
Multiple users can edit the same document simultaneously and see each other's changes live
The document is updated only when it is re-uploaded
Changes are saved only when the document is closed
questions.answer: Multiple users can edit the same document simultaneously and see each other's changes live
questions.explanation: Google Workspace tools support real-time collaboration, allowing multiple contributors to work simultaneously with live updates visible to all editors.

questions.question: True or False: The answer to "The SIFT method for evaluating online information stands for:" is: Search, Identify, Find, Test.
questions.explanation: False. The correct answer is: Stop, Investigate the source, Find better coverage, Trace claims.

questions.question: Which of the following best protects an account in addition to a strong password?
options: Sharing the password with a trusted friend
Using the same password across all accounts
Writing the password on a sticky note
questions.explanation: Two-factor authentication adds a second verification step (e.g., a code sent to your phone), making it significantly harder for attackers to access your account even if they know your password.

questions.question: A professional LinkedIn headline should:
options: Be your full legal name only
List every job you have ever had
Briefly describe your professional identity and value
Include your phone number and home address
questions.answer: Briefly describe your professional identity and value
questions.explanation: A LinkedIn headline is a short pitch that appears under your name. It should communicate who you are professionally and what you offer.

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of descriptive analytics?
options: Predicting next month's sales using a model
Calculating the average customer age from a dataset
Recommending products based on past behavior
questions.answer: Calculating the average customer age from a dataset
questions.explanation: Descriptive analytics summarizes historical data (e.g., averages, totals, distributions) to describe what has happened.

questions.question: True or False: The answer to "In project management, a Kanban board organizes tasks into columns such as:" is: To Do, In Progress, Done.
questions.explanation: True. To Do, In Progress, Done is correct.

questions.question: Which e-commerce business model charges customers a recurring fee for ongoing access to a product or service?
questions.explanation: Subscription models (e.g., Netflix, Spotify) generate recurring revenue by charging customers a regular fee — monthly or annually — for continued access.

questions.question: Which of the following tasks is most likely to be automated by AI in the near future?
options: Sorting and categorizing large volumes of data
questions.answer: Sorting and categorizing large volumes of data
questions.explanation: Repetitive, rule-based tasks like data sorting are well-suited for automation. Creative, emotional, and ethical tasks remain difficult for AI to replicate.

questions.question: Which formula would count only the cells in B1:B20 that contain numerical values (not blank or text)?
questions.explanation: =COUNT counts only cells containing numbers. =COUNTA counts all non-empty cells including text.

questions.question: True or False: The answer to "A pivot table in a spreadsheet is used to:" is: Automatically delete duplicate rows.
questions.explanation: False. The correct answer is: Summarize, group, and analyze large datasets interactively.

questions.question: The gig economy refers to:
options: A market of short-term, freelance, or contract-based work enabled by digital platforms
Government regulation of the tech industry
questions.answer: A market of short-term, freelance, or contract-based work enabled by digital platforms
questions.explanation: The gig economy encompasses platforms like Uber, Fiverr, and Upwork, where workers take on flexible, project-based work rather than traditional employment.

questions.question: You are asked to analyze sales data for a small business. Describe the steps you would take in a spreadsheet to (1) calculate total and average monthly sales, (2) identify the top 3 months by revenue, and (3) create a visual to present your findings.
questions.answer: (1) Use =SUM() for total sales and =AVERAGE() for average monthly sales. (2) Sort the revenue column in descending order or use LARGE() to find the top 3 values. (3) Select the monthly data and insert a bar or column chart, add a title and axis labels, to clearly visualize revenue trends.
questions.explanation: Effective data analysis in a spreadsheet combines formulas for calculation, sorting/filtering for ranking, and charts for visual communication.

questions.question: Describe two ways that AI is currently changing the workplace and explain one skill that you believe will remain important for humans even as AI advances. Justify your answer.
questions.answer: AI changes: (1) automating repetitive tasks such as data entry, scheduling, and customer service chatbots, freeing workers for higher-level work; (2) enabling data-driven decision-making by quickly analyzing large datasets for insights. Enduring human skill: critical thinking / creativity — AI can process information but struggles to evaluate ethical implications, generate truly original ideas, or navigate complex human relationships. These require human judgment and context.
questions.explanation: While AI excels at pattern recognition and automation, skills like creativity, ethical reasoning, and interpersonal communication remain uniquely human strengths.

quizQuestions.question: Which of the following is an example of software?
quizQuestions.explanation: Software is a set of programs or instructions. Google Chrome is an application (software); the others are hardware.

quizQuestions.question: What does RAM stand for?
quizQuestions.explanation: RAM (Random Access Memory) is the computer's short-term memory that holds data currently in use by the processor.

quizQuestions.question: True or False: The answer to "Which of the following is an example of software" is: Google Chrome browser.
quizQuestions.explanation: True. Google Chrome browser is correct.

quizQuestions.question: Fill in the blank: Complete: In the context of the topic, ___ refers to "which of the following is an input device."
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Mouse.

quizQuestions.question: Which greeting is most appropriate for a professional email to a potential employer?
quizQuestions.explanation: Professional emails use formal greetings. 'Dear [Title Last Name],' is the standard respectful opening.

quizQuestions.question: What is the purpose of the CC field in an email?
options: To send a copy to additional recipients who should be aware of the message
To attach files to the email
quizQuestions.answer: To send a copy to additional recipients who should be aware of the message
quizQuestions.explanation: CC (Carbon Copy) sends a visible copy to other recipients, signaling they are included for awareness but are not the primary audience.

quizQuestions.question: Which of the following is the best subject line for a professional email?
quizQuestions.explanation: A good subject line is specific and informative, telling the recipient exactly what the email is about at a glance.

quizQuestions.question: True or False: The answer to "Which greeting is most appropriate for a professional email to a potential employer" is: Dear Mr. Kim,.
quizQuestions.explanation: True. Dear Mr. Kim, is correct.

quizQuestions.question: What is the purpose of using heading styles (Heading 1, Heading 2) in a document?
options: To change the document's page orientation
To create consistent formatting and enable a navigable outline
To insert a table of images
quizQuestions.answer: To create consistent formatting and enable a navigable outline
quizQuestions.explanation: Heading styles apply consistent formatting and allow document navigation tools and automatic table of contents generation.

quizQuestions.question: Which keyboard shortcut is used to make selected text bold in most word processors?
quizQuestions.explanation: Ctrl + B (Cmd + B on Mac) applies bold formatting to selected text in Google Docs, Microsoft Word, and most word processors.

quizQuestions.question: What does 'margin' refer to in document design?
options: The font size used in the document
The blank space around the edges of a page
The number of words per line
The color of the page background
quizQuestions.answer: The blank space around the edges of a page
quizQuestions.explanation: Margins are the white space borders on all four sides of a page that frame the content and improve readability.

quizQuestions.question: Fill in the blank: Complete: In the context of the topic, ___ refers to "which line spacing setting provides the most white space between lines, improving readability for longer documents."
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Double (2.0).

quizQuestions.question: In a spreadsheet, a cell reference like 'B3' refers to:
quizQuestions.explanation: Cell references use the column letter followed by the row number. B3 is the cell at Column B, Row 3.

quizQuestions.question: What does the =COUNT(A1:A10) formula do?
options: Adds all values in A1 to A10
Counts the number of cells in A1:A10 that contain numbers
Finds the largest value in A1:A10
quizQuestions.answer: Counts the number of cells in A1:A10 that contain numbers
quizQuestions.explanation: =COUNT counts only cells with numeric data in the specified range.

quizQuestions.question: What is an absolute cell reference in a spreadsheet (e.g., $A$1)?
options: A cell reference that changes when you copy a formula to another cell
A cell reference that stays fixed regardless of where the formula is copied
A reference to a cell in another spreadsheet file
A cell that cannot be edited
quizQuestions.answer: A cell reference that stays fixed regardless of where the formula is copied
quizQuestions.explanation: Adding $ signs locks the column and/or row reference. $A$1 always points to cell A1 no matter where the formula is moved.

quizQuestions.question: The formula =IF(B2>100, 'Over Budget', 'OK') will display 'Over Budget' when:
quizQuestions.explanation: The IF function checks the logical condition (B2>100). If true, it returns 'Over Budget'; otherwise it returns 'OK'.

quizQuestions.question: What does sorting data in a spreadsheet allow you to do?
options: Arrange data in ascending or descending order based on a column
Change the font of all cells
quizQuestions.answer: Arrange data in ascending or descending order based on a column
quizQuestions.explanation: Sorting reorders rows based on values in a selected column, either from A to Z (ascending) or Z to A (descending).

quizQuestions.question: What does a filter in a spreadsheet do?
options: Changes the color of cells based on value
Hides rows that do not meet specified criteria, making it easier to focus on relevant data
Removes all formulas from a range
Copies data to a new sheet
quizQuestions.answer: Hides rows that do not meet specified criteria, making it easier to focus on relevant data
quizQuestions.explanation: Filters temporarily hide rows that don't match your criteria, allowing you to view and analyze a subset of your data.

quizQuestions.question: True or False: The answer to "What does sorting data in a spreadsheet allow you to do" is: Arrange data in ascending or descending order based on a column.
quizQuestions.explanation: True. Arrange data in ascending or descending order based on a column is correct.

quizQuestions.question: True or False: The answer to "What does a filter in a spreadsheet do" is: Changes the color of cells based on value.
quizQuestions.explanation: False. The correct answer is: Hides rows that do not meet specified criteria, making it easier to focus on relevant data.

quizQuestions.question: Which of the following is a best practice for slide text?
options: Include complete paragraphs so the audience can read everything
Use bullet points with brief phrases, not full sentences
Use 3 different fonts per slide for variety
Avoid using any text and rely only on images
quizQuestions.answer: Use bullet points with brief phrases, not full sentences
quizQuestions.explanation: Slides should support the speaker, not replace them. Brief bullet points keep slides clean and the audience focused on the presenter.

quizQuestions.question: A slide's color palette should ideally consist of:
options: As many colors as possible to look creative
A different color for every slide
quizQuestions.explanation: A limited, consistent color palette creates visual cohesion and professionalism. Too many colors look chaotic and distract from the content.

quizQuestions.question: What is the purpose of a title slide in a presentation?
options: To list every topic that will be covered in detail
To introduce the topic, presenter, and context to the audience
To display the data and conclusions
To show the references and sources used
quizQuestions.answer: To introduce the topic, presenter, and context to the audience
quizQuestions.explanation: The title slide sets the stage — it shows the presentation topic, the presenter's name, and often the date or organization.

quizQuestions.question: True or False: The answer to "Which of the following is a best practice for slide text" is: Use bullet points with brief phrases, not full sentences.
quizQuestions.explanation: True. Use bullet points with brief phrases, not full sentences is correct.

quizQuestions.question: Which of the following is a benefit of using cloud storage for team projects?
options: Only one person can work on a file at a time
Files can be accessed and edited from any device with internet access
Files are permanently deleted after 30 days
Cloud storage does not require an internet connection
quizQuestions.answer: Files can be accessed and edited from any device with internet access
quizQuestions.explanation: Cloud storage enables access from any internet-connected device, supporting remote collaboration and eliminating the need to email files back and forth.

quizQuestions.question: What does 'version history' allow you to do in Google Docs?
options: Delete all previous versions of a document
See who made changes and restore earlier versions of the document
Share the document with new users
Convert the document to a spreadsheet
quizQuestions.answer: See who made changes and restore earlier versions of the document
quizQuestions.explanation: Version history tracks every saved change, letting collaborators see who edited what and when, and restore earlier versions if needed.

quizQuestions.question: True or False: The answer to "Which of the following is a benefit of using cloud storage for team projects" is: Files can be accessed and edited from any device with internet access.
quizQuestions.explanation: True. Files can be accessed and edited from any device with internet access is correct.

quizQuestions.question: Which Boolean search operator would you use to exclude a word from search results?
quizQuestions.explanation: The Boolean operator NOT (or a minus sign in Google) excludes terms from results. E.g., 'jaguar NOT car' returns results about the animal, not the vehicle.

quizQuestions.question: Which domain extension is most commonly associated with government websites in the United States?
quizQuestions.explanation: The .gov domain is restricted to U.S. government entities, making it a reliable indicator of official government information.

quizQuestions.question: Which of the following is the best indicator that a website is a reliable source?
options: It cites peer-reviewed studies and identifies its authors
It appears at the top of Google search results
It has been shared many times on social media
quizQuestions.answer: It cites peer-reviewed studies and identifies its authors
quizQuestions.explanation: Credible sources identify their authors, cite verifiable evidence, and have editorial oversight — not simply high traffic or eye-catching design.

quizQuestions.question: True or False: The answer to "Which Boolean search operator would you use to exclude a word from search results" is: NOT.
quizQuestions.explanation: True. NOT is correct.
